skill.name: ai-video-generation
skill.description: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI. Models: Veo 3.1, Veo 3, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse 1.0, Wan 2.5, Grok Imagine Video, OmniHuman, Fabric, HunyuanVideo. Capabilities: text-to-video, image-to-video, reference-to-video, video editing, lipsync, avatar animation, video upscaling, foley sound. Use for: social media videos, marketing content, explainer videos, product demos, AI avatars. Triggers: video generation, ai video, text to video, image to video, veo, animate image, video from image, ai animation, video generator, generate video, t2v, i2v, ai video maker, create video with ai, runway alternative, pika alternative, sora alternative, kling alternative, seedance, happyhorse
skill.summary: Generate AI videos with Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, Grok and 40+ models via inference.sh CLI
skill.author: inference-skills

security_audit.summary: Security audit complete. Static findings are false positives: external_commands detected are markdown code blocks, network URLs point to legitimate inference.sh API service, and no actual cryptographic code exists. This is a legitimate documentation file for an AI video generation skill.

medium_findings.title: Static Pattern Detections Are Documentation Examples
medium_findings.description: The 59 external_command detections and 19 network URL detections are all false positives. The 'external_commands' are markdown code fences (```bash blocks) showing example CLI commands. The 'network' URLs are legitimate inference.sh service endpoints and example media URLs, which are expected for a video generation skill. No actual code execution or credential exfiltration occurs.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed full SKILL.md content - all detections are documentation patterns, not executable code or security vulnerabilities

content.value_statement: Creating AI videos requires managing multiple model APIs and complex video generation workflows. This skill provides a unified CLI interface to generate videos using Google Veo, Seedance 2.0, HappyHorse, Wan, and 40+ other AI video models.
actual_capabilities: Generate videos from text prompts using 40+ AI models including Google Veo 3.1, Seedance 2.0, and HappyHorse
Animate static images into videos with Wan 2.5, Seedance 2.0 I2V, and HappyHorse I2V
Create talking avatars with lip sync using OmniHuman, Fabric, and PixVerse Lipsync
Edit existing videos using natural language prompts with HappyHorse Video Edit
Upscale video quality and add foley sound effects to generated videos
Generate reference-to-video content using character or style references
limitations: Requires inference.sh CLI (belt) installation and authentication before use
Some models have duration limits (HappyHorse max 15s for base tier, 1080P requires higher tier)
Video generation is credit-based; some models require paid inference.sh account
Output quality depends on prompt engineering; complex scenes may need multiple iterations

use_cases.title: Create Marketing Videos at Scale
use_cases.description: Generate product demo videos, social media content, and promotional clips using text prompts. Perfect for marketing teams needing quick video turnaround without video editing expertise.

use_cases.title: Build AI Video Automation Pipelines
use_cases.description: Integrate AI video generation into workflows using CLI commands. Developers can chain video generation with image creation, text-to-speech, and social media posting for automated content pipelines.

use_cases.title: Create AI Avatar Presentations
use_cases.description: Generate talking head videos from portraits and audio files using OmniHuman or Fabric. Ideal for creating personalized video messages, training content, or multilingual presentations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text-to-Video Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to create a video. Generate a {duration}-second clip of {scene_description} using the Veo 3.1 Fast model.
prompt_templates.scenario: For beginners wanting to create simple AI videos

prompt_templates.title: Image-to-Video Animation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to animate an image. Run the Wan 2.5 I2V model with the image at {image_url} and prompt: {animation_description}
prompt_templates.scenario: For animating product photos or portraits

prompt_templates.title: AI Avatar with Lip Sync
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to create a talking avatar. Run Fabric 1.0 with image_url: {portrait_url} and audio_url: {speech_audio_url}
prompt_templates.scenario: For creating talking head videos from photos and audio

prompt_templates.title: Automated Video Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the ai-video-generation skill to create a complete video: 1) Generate base video with {model} showing {scene}, 2) Upscale using Topaz, 3) Add foley sound with {sound_prompt}
prompt_templates.scenario: For advanced users building automated content pipelines

output_examples.input: Generate a drone shot flying over a mountain range at sunset with cinematic lighting
output_examples.output: A 5-second AI-generated video showing aerial footage of mountains with warm sunset colors, smooth camera movement, and dramatic lighting

output_examples.input: Animate a product photo with the product floating in space with particle effects
output_examples.output: A product video showing the item rotating slowly in a 3D space environment with floating particles and soft lighting

output_examples.input: Create a talking avatar from a portrait and audio recording explaining quarterly results
output_examples.output: A video of the person in the portrait speaking with natural lip movements synced to the audio narration

best_practices: Start with simple, descriptive prompts and iterate based on results before adding complexity
Use reference images when available to guide style and character consistency in generation
Chain multiple operations: generate video, upscale, then add sound effects for polished final output
Test different models for the same prompt to find the best quality-speed tradeoff for your use case
anti_patterns: Do not expect instant results - video generation typically takes 30-120 seconds per clip depending on model
Avoid overly complex prompts with many conflicting elements - focus on one main action or scene
Do not skip authentication - belt login is required before running any video generation commands

faq.question: What models are available for text-to-video generation?
faq.answer: Available models include Google Veo 3.1 (Fast), Veo 3, Seedance 2.0 T2V, HappyHorse 1.0 T2V, WAN-T2V, and Grok Video. Each has different quality-speed tradeoffs.

faq.question: How do I create talking avatar videos?
faq.answer: Use OmniHuman for multi-character scenes or Fabric 1.0 for single character lip sync. Provide an image_url of a portrait and audio_url of speech audio.

faq.question: What video formats are supported for input?
faq.answer: Video editing accepts MP4 and MOV formats via URL. Image-to-video accepts JPG and PNG. Output is MP4 format.

faq.question: How long can generated videos be?
faq.answer: Duration limits vary by model: HappyHorse supports up to 15s, Seedance 2.0 supports 5-10s, Veo models support various durations. Check model documentation for specifics.

faq.question: Do I need to pay for video generation?
faq.answer: The inference.sh CLI is free to install. Video generation uses credits - some basic models have free tiers while premium models require paid accounts.

faq.question: Can I edit videos I already generated?
faq.answer: Yes, use HappyHorse Video Edit to modify existing videos with natural language prompts like changing backgrounds, adding effects, or extending scenes.
